reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: Great little pump
reviews.body: Took this camping and worked fantastic. I needed the hose longer, so I put another one on the end and taping it to make sure it didn't come apart. I put the pump in a mesh bag and tied a rope on it, and another on to the on off switch, because the stream has a big washed out edge making the length of the hose short. It worked better than I expected. I filled the 5 gallon buckets and took showers with it. We go wilderness camping, not another soak around.

reviews.title: Love this thing

I brought a five gallon bucket to use as my water container. I'll update about its longevity once I've used it a few times.

reviews.title: Great pump, lots of pressure
reviews.body: This is a great pump! We use this pump in a homemade water heater in our camper van. The water pressure is very strong. Having the battery/power switch outside the water is super convenient so I can easily turn the pump off and on without having to pull the pump out of the water. The small sprayer is convenient because it has an on/off lever that allows you to moderate, or stop, water flow without turning off the pump. The shower head is a nice spray but I'd like it better if it also had an on/off lever. I can't speak to longevity because we've only had it a month or so but so far, it's far exceeded my needs and expectations.

reviews.title: Poor quality.  Complicated set up.
reviews.body: I purchased this product to replace another camp shower head thinking that it would be better based on product reviews and the design of the pump.  Boy was I wrong!  Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

reviews.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: New battery pack style difficult to install and remove
reviews.body: 11/2022 Update:  Unfortunately the company has changed the design of the battery packs and the new style is difficult to remove and install on the camp shower.  I will not be purchasing more.  3 battery packs have failed of the 4 that I own.  One more star removed from this review.Original review: I use this in my house call pet grooming business, to help mix and distribute shampoo and conditioner onto the pets, as well as rinse them.  I replaced the opaque hose with a clear hose (from local hardware store), and I don't use the sprayer nozzle (as it frightens the pets), I just wrap a rubberized hair band around the end of the hose to make it easier to grasp.  I like having the clear hose, as it makes it easier to make sure the hose is getting dry and not growing mold. I use it at least once or twice a week and it has lasted two years before one of the battery packs failed (it would be nice to be able to buy battery packs separately). The pump is quiet, and efficient at delivering the product/rinse water onto the pets.  I have bought 2 units to have spare batteries around, although one battery pack can usually last through one large dog.  I like to have back up chargers in my car and house, and back up batteries on hand in my grooming kit.  I don't leave the batteries out in the car heat and freezing weather, but bring them into the house to protect the battery life. One star off because I would really love it if they could make the black cord that attaches to the battery pack longer by about 12" (and a clear hose and housing and sprayer head would be awesome!) I actually link this product on my business Facebook page to recommend to my clients and friends for bathing their own dogs.  I have gotten feedback that it is also a great toy for the the grandkids, for washing cars, and camping!

reviews.title: Gets the job done... barely
reviews.body: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

The two thing we hated was that that cord that’s attached to the motor was not long enough for a 8 gallon bucket. And the hose was not long enough to reach the top of the tent. Buy one with a longer cord and hose.

reviews.title: This product would be great if the on/off switch worked
reviews.body: I really liked this shower the first 2 uses, but on the third use the on off switch quit working. I had to unplug the battery unit to turn the shower off. This was tricky since I needed to shut it off before the water level in my bucket got too low and would burn up the motor. I would be happy if I could get a replacement battery pack/switch.

reviews.title: Gets the job done... barely
reviews.body: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

Used it outdoors twice and it worked as it should ... then, it would again turn on and stay on until you pull the pump out of the water.  As for the power, I used the supplied cigarette light adapter, that also was glitchy as well, it would turn on and then stop working but you can hear the pump working but no water, so you would have to pull it out of the power socket and plug it back in and then the water will flow again .... totally not worth the headache with this!!!  dont' waste your $$ on this and buy something else that is better build!!!

reviews.title: Bad and very cheaply made on/off switch
reviews.body: Both of the batteries with the on/off button broke-down after about 5 or 6 use and now we have to keep disconnecting the power cord from the pump in the middle of the use to stop the water !!!! Otherwise the pump doesn’t stop !!!!!!

reviews.title: It could be better...
reviews.body: So far it works, with a few issues. 1) The cord on the battery is too short. Makes it very inconvenient to turn off and on while showering. 2) The on/off button is kind of a pain. You have to hold it down for about 4 seconds to turn it on each time you turn it off. In the middle of a shower it's annoying. 3) The biggest problem we have is that there is no way to control the spray volume or pressure. It puts out plenty of water but it goes through 5 gallons really fast. It's a challenge to get 2 decent showers from 5 gallons of water. If you have an unlimited supply of water then I guess it would be fine. 4) The clip that holds the shower head is poorly designed. If you unclip it during your shower, reclipping it is a hassle... especially if you have soap in your eyes!All-in-all it's OK, but it could be a lot better. I'm sorry we paid $40 for this product.

reviews.title: Great, but needs a few modifications to be perfect
reviews.body: Since you're not supposed to get the battery wet, I found myself putting it in a ziplock when using. The power cord needs to be longer to get it out of the way. The way to turn on this thing is to hold the button on the battery which makes zero sense considering you're not supposed to get the battery wet. They need to provide a waterproof power switch somewhere else. Also, make the shower tube longer. I have to bend down to keep the pump at the bottom of the bucket.

reviews.title: it does work
reviews.body: followed the instruction, charged the batteries until stop flashing,  it would turn on, however no water pumped out.updatedcharged the battery overnight on a wall usb outlet anyway. turn it on, the water pump out from the hose, still no water from the shower head. clean the shower head with small needle. It finally works.check the battery status, it indicates 50% power level, even with overnight charge.

reviews.title: It uses a lot of water fast
reviews.body: Just a heads up it goes through about 2 gallons in 3 minuets if used consistently if you are being water conscious your best bet is to wet yourself down real fast. Try to keep as much of that water that has no soap in it to go back into whatever bucket it was coming out of and then turn it off soap yourself down turn it back on rinse yourself off for a few secondsturn it off scrub what you have left on your soap wise so you bring it all to the surface and then then rinse off a second time possibly a third but do it in phases of about 15 to 30 seconds for the whole process. It sucks because you have to keep turning the on and off button on and off ha ha ha, which I feel like with the amount of water, moisture, and soap on hands could lead to future issues with the apparatus. If water is not an issue and you’re close to a water source than this thing is clutch

reviews.title: Not for me.
reviews.body: I wouldn't buy it again. The start button is difficult to press, and it's bulky and inconvenient to use when you're actually showering.

reviews.body: I was excited to get this. First time worked well, but tube does not stay connected. Tape will not help it. Have to keep untwisting the hose as well. Keeps kinking up. But after 6 times it just completely quit working. Cannot get it to stop kinking so water does not go through to shower head. Great idea but poorly made and waste of money! reviews.title: Product arrived used
reviews.body: I began return process and I'm a week away from my camping trip I hope I find a good shower head since I received this one used. Found hair and calcium already built up in the creases.

reviews.title: Poor quality.  Complicated set up.
reviews.body: I purchased this product to replace another camp shower head thinking that it would be better based on product reviews and the design of the pump.  Boy was I wrong!  Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

reviews.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: Bought two: one did not work
reviews.body: One of the two I ordered did not work.  Its motor ran but just created turbulence in the water source without actually moving any water toward the shower head.

reviews.title: Cord is to short, buy one with a longer cord and hose.
reviews.body: I bought this and forgot I had it. We went camping last weekend and took it with us. We loved that it came with two batteries. We loved that it worked, and worked well. The two thing we hated was that that cord that’s attached to the motor was not long enough for a 8 gallon bucket. And the hose was not long enough to reach the top of the tent. Buy one with a longer cord and hose.

reviews.title: It could be better...
reviews.body: So far it works, with a few issues. 1) The cord on the battery is too short. Makes it very inconvenient to turn off and on while showering. 2) The on/off button is kind of a pain. You have to hold it down for about 4 seconds to turn it on each time you turn it off. In the middle of a shower it's annoying. 3) The biggest problem we have is that there is no way to control the spray volume or pressure. It puts out plenty of water but it goes through 5 gallons really fast. It's a challenge to get 2 decent showers from 5 gallons of water. If you have an unlimited supply of water then I guess it would be fine. 4) The clip that holds the shower head is poorly designed. If you unclip it during your shower, reclipping it is a hassle... especially if you have soap in your eyes!All-in-all it's OK, but it could be a lot better. I'm sorry we paid $40 for this product.

reviews.title: Good but hand nozzle leaks
reviews.body: Good but the hand nozzle leaks where the hose enters it. NO way that I can see to fix it.

reviews.title: New battery pack style difficult to install and remove
reviews.body: 11/2022 Update:  Unfortunately the company has changed the design of the battery packs and the new style is difficult to remove and install on the camp shower.  I will not be purchasing more.  3 battery packs have failed of the 4 that I own.  One more star removed from this review.Original review: I use this in my house call pet grooming business, to help mix and distribute shampoo and conditioner onto the pets, as well as rinse them.  I replaced the opaque hose with a clear hose (from local hardware store), and I don't use the sprayer nozzle (as it frightens the pets), I just wrap a rubberized hair band around the end of the hose to make it easier to grasp.  I like having the clear hose, as it makes it easier to make sure the hose is getting dry and not growing mold. I use it at least once or twice a week and it has lasted two years before one of the battery packs failed (it would be nice to be able to buy battery packs separately). The pump is quiet, and efficient at delivering the product/rinse water onto the pets.  I have bought 2 units to have spare batteries around, although one battery pack can usually last through one large dog.  I like to have back up chargers in my car and house, and back up batteries on hand in my grooming kit.  I don't leave the batteries out in the car heat and freezing weather, but bring them into the house to protect the battery life. One star off because I would really love it if they could make the black cord that attaches to the battery pack longer by about 12" (and a clear hose and housing and sprayer head would be awesome!) I actually link this product on my business Facebook page to recommend to my clients and friends for bathing their own dogs.  I have gotten feedback that it is also a great toy for the the grandkids, for washing cars, and camping!

reviews.title: Gets the job done... barely
reviews.body: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.body: I was excited to receive this product to make our trips to the beach easier to manage. On the second run of this device the power button began malfunctioning and would not turn off. I was really hoping this could last me longer than two trips to the beach but unfortunately it did not.

reviews.title: Junk - Didn't Even Last One Camping Trip
reviews.body: This was great while it worked - we got maybe 5 showers out of it.Took it camping, lasted 3 days - 5 showers. First it wouldn't shut off, then it just wouldn't take charge or run. After all the fuss, it was just easier to heat water in buckets and use a pot of water to wet, wash and rinse in a shower tent.This product was a TOTAL disappointment & waste of money.

reviews.title: Broke on me after 1 time!
reviews.body: Broke after second use! It was very hard to figure out how to charge it as well.  I finally did figure it out, and charged both before use for hours.The first time was mediocre water pressure and I used it for probably 5 minutes.  The second time it didn’t even have anything more than a tiny sprinkle of water.  Junk!  I’m still waiting on my refund.

reviews.title: Totally not worth it!
reviews.body: 1st purchased this product at around Aug and it was basically defective out the box, it would turn on and stay on even you press the power button off. returned and replaced .. 2nd one came in and tested at home in sink and it was working like it should. Used it outdoors twice and it worked as it should ... then, it would again turn on and stay on until you pull the pump out of the water.  As for the power, I used the supplied cigarette light adapter, that also was glitchy as well, it would turn on and then stop working but you can hear the pump working but no water, so you would have to pull it out of the power socket and plug it back in and then the water will flow again .... totally not worth the headache with this!!!  dont' waste your $$ on this and buy something else that is better build!!!

reviews.title: Did not work
reviews.body: Did not suction the water did not work. Need to return it

reviews.title: Battery only works for about 10 seconds then is dead.
reviews.body: This never worked for longer than 10 seconds from the time I purchased it. And I never received two batteries. Very disappointed for the money I paid. ☹

reviews.title: dangerous! it falls when being hanged on a tree.
reviews.body: dangerous as it fell down on my child's head. because the design of the hook is not enough to hold tge whole shower head, it falls forward. I contacted them and they asked me for pictures. i already packed camping and headed back home. went back to reality like work so I cant have pictures. i don't need refund. I just want them to know their product can be better.

reviews.title: Do not buy this product
reviews.body: Water pump is not strong enough to provide flow. Water came at a rrivkle or not at all at times. Tried to return but I waited too long. Avoid buying this product.

reviews.title: charging cable stopped working
reviews.body: this product was as described but without the charging cable working the entire system is useless. I really needed this to work, it's very unfortunate. It worked to charge some of one pack then stopped, i tried charging it in a car as well as with 5v or below charging ports. Never charged it above the 5v as instructed. very disappointed.

reviews.title: Failed When Needed It
reviews.body: We bought this for use during power outages. We even bought a spare battery. Both batteries though plugged in prior to the storm and allegedly charged failed to operate when we went to use them. Forget this electric garbage and get yourself a good old fashioned hanging bag style.  (And no you can't use the name 'Electric Garbage' for your band name I already called it!)

reviews.title: Faulty
reviews.body: I tested mine in the bathroom which seemed to work well. Took it out camping the next day and the motor was already broken. Was really disappointed in the product, and the company’s customer service were no help. Thankfully Amazon were cool and gave me a refund.

reviews.title: Garbage, don't waste your money
reviews.body: What a waste of money, sent it back

reviews.title: Battery does not hold charge past 1 day
reviews.body: Bought this to go camping. Charged it and tried it an hour after charge and it worked. However, after I charged it and then waited to use it a day later there was no power. Absolutely, do not buy this product as it is a piece of crap. Also, warranty is awful and they will not let me return it. Lost my money.

reviews.title: Product is cheap
reviews.body: We bought these, and by the time we went to use them. It was to late to return them. They don’t work! We charged the batteries over night. Went to use them on a camping trip. Wouldn’t work!

reviews.title: DO NOT BUY!!!
reviews.body: DO NOT BUY THIS!!!The cord has a short in it after using one time.I purchased it in September but just opened it last week and its already not working properly. Amazon says my return window has closed because of the timeframe.

reviews.title: Product arrived used
reviews.body: I began return process and I'm a week away from my camping trip I hope I find a good shower head since I received this one used. Found hair and calcium already built up in the creases.

reviews.title: Don’t waste your money
reviews.body: It doesn’t work, literally zero water comes out. Turns on, makes the noises, but no water. Waste of money cheap piece of garbage.

reviews.title: Used, not new
reviews.body: The supposedly brand new shower was covered in a mysterious white powdery substance revealing it has been used before and as such had to be immediately returned.

reviews.title: No USB Charger
reviews.body: I think I got a returned item.  Box was open and there wasn't a USB charger included.Totally worthless without a way to charge it.Messed up my camping trip this weekend......

reviews.title: Oh well
reviews.body: The batteries charged up just fine but unfortunately I am having no luck getting it to pump water. It sounds like it is trying really hard but sadly it’s not pumping water. I guess I got a dud. Will return it for a replacement.

reviews.title: Poor quality.  Complicated set up.
reviews.body: I purchased this product to replace another camp shower head thinking that it would be better based on product reviews and the design of the pump.  Boy was I wrong!  Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

reviews.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: Batteries Never Worked total bummer
reviews.body: I bought this shower months ago to use at our cabin in the woods during our traditional Thanksgiving dinner. Tried to use it for dishes and neither battery worked and also would not charge. I used a proper charger as stated. I would love to be able to return it but didnt even try to use it till recent. Any suggestions or if this seller would replace the batteries. Not sure what to do. Be careful and test is as soon as you get it as to not end up like me.

reviews.title: Worked great  1st weekend then died company was great to work with
reviews.body: When the pump 1st arrived it was great.  We used it maybe 6 times  then the pump died.  Myself and my husband read and followed  the instructions to be sure we used and charged it properly.  Would like to add I reached out to the company and they responded right away and offered an exchange or refund.  They were very easy to work with.

reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: Great little pump
reviews.body: Took this camping and worked fantastic. I needed the hose longer, so I put another one on the end and taping it to make sure it didn't come apart. I put the pump in a mesh bag and tied a rope on it, and another on to the on off switch, because the stream has a big washed out edge making the length of the hose short. It worked better than I expected. I filled the 5 gallon buckets and took showers with it. We go wilderness camping, not another soak around.

reviews.title: Works great, just get it.
reviews.body: Works perfectly and set up is quick and easy. We've used it for showering while camping and also for washing dishes while camping. I've also used it for watering plants on my porch, works great.

reviews.title: Love this thing
reviews.body: This thing is amazing. I brought it to a camping music festival in the middle of summer and everyone was so jealous! I was able to take a cool shower every single day and did not fully kill one battery. I even stared it a few times with others. It was probably used for 30-45 minutes over the course of four days and did not die. Even if it did, I still has a backup battery, which is great. I brought a five gallon bucket to use as my water container. I'll update about its longevity once I've used it a few times.

reviews.title: Enough battery to last us couple of days in festival
reviews.body: Worked great for our camping trip. Went to Big Dub 2026 and going again this year. This worked for us to use as aportable shower in a shower tent for about 7 people in our group. This year will be using this again.

reviews.title: Great pump, lots of pressure
reviews.body: This is a great pump! We use this pump in a homemade water heater in our camper van. The water pressure is very strong. Having the battery/power switch outside the water is super convenient so I can easily turn the pump off and on without having to pull the pump out of the water. The small sprayer is convenient because it has an on/off lever that allows you to moderate, or stop, water flow without turning off the pump. The shower head is a nice spray but I'd like it better if it also had an on/off lever. I can't speak to longevity because we've only had it a month or so but so far, it's far exceeded my needs and expectations.

reviews.title: Love the shower
reviews.body: Just absolutely love the shower it makes it very convenient currently my hot water tank is out and so I'm having to keep my water and shower using a bucket and hauling my water in until it gets fixed and this has been a godsend. Functional.
